summary:
Plain X-rays are less useful than other modalities like CT scans and MRIs because while providing a first look at alignment problems, they lack detail necessary for comprehensive evaluation including soft tissue structures crucial for accurate diagnoses such as identifying specific types of slippage involving nerves or discs which require more advanced imaging methods. Thus among options provided, plain xray would be considered least helpful overall given its limitations particularly regarding sensitivity towards detecting fine anatomical details needed beyond simple skeletal assessments alone without additional context from higher quality images capturing full spectrum pathology involved typically seen through modern radiological practices today. Agent1 concludes based on updated understanding emphasizing necessity of complementary diagnostic tools alongside primary but limited view offered via standard xrays only suitable under certain
